title: ISO20022GuarantyTypeValues
description: Specifies the type of credit enhancement. (ISO20022)

x-enumeration-literals.name: Collateral
x-enumeration-literals.description: Security is guaranteed by collateral, ie, assets pledged to a lender until a loan is repaid.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Government
x-enumeration-literals.description: Security is guaranteed by the government.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Lien
x-enumeration-literals.description: Security is guaranteed by a lien, ie, a creditor's claim against property, for example, a mortgage is a lien against a house.

x-enumeration-literals.name: LetterOfCredit
x-enumeration-literals.description: Security is guaranteed by a letter of credit, ie, a document issued by a bank guaranteeing the payment up to a stated amount for a specified period.

x-enumeration-literals.name: NegativePledge
x-enumeration-literals.description: Security has a negative pledge, ie, the issuer will not pledge any assets if doing so would result in less security for the investors.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Insurance
x-enumeration-literals.description: Security is guaranteed by an insurance policy.
